prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of pyridine (11 atoms: N, C×5, H×5) computed at the DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') using PySCF (RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion state: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz, not re-optimised at this level (single-point). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Fukui indices computed by finite difference on Mulliken charges between the N, N+1, and N−1 electron states.

Headline result: the most electrophilic site is N (atom 1, 1-based) with f⁺ = 0.48422572. The most nucleophilic site is C4 (atom 4) with f⁻ = 0.11826. Charge conservation confirmed: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true). No engine warnings were raised. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/.../pyridine.xyz. Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260703-202414_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
